macro_summary: Monday's session saw the S&P 500 slip 0.3% to 718.01 as Iran missile reports near the Strait of Hormuz pushed WTI up 2.4% to $104.12 and sent the VIX higher by 7.7% to 18.29. The 10-year yield rose 7bp to 4.45%, reinforcing sticky inflation fears and pushing back rate-cut timelines. Late earnings from Palantir showed Q1 revenue surging 85% YoY, offering a bright spot amid broad caution.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $104.12, clawing back from Monday's spike above $106 as the market digested UAE missile detections and US Central Command denials. Gold edged down 0.2% to $4,540.20, holding its safe-haven bid but retreating from session highs. The DXY was flat at 98.50, with the US10Y jump widening the rate differential.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains in a holding pattern. US10Y at 4.45% keeps the NIM compression narrative firmly in place, with the MAS S$NEER stance offering no relief. DBS is the only name above the 50-day but is hard-blocked on extension, while OCBC and UOB hover near their averages with no imminent catalysts.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression persists, no relief in sight
singapore.key_development: US10Y climbed 7bp to 4.45%, reinforcing the dual NIM compression channels and extending the cautious stance on all three banks.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster remains uniformly extended. ASML is the sole name below its 50-day after a sharp drop, but the distorted R:R keeps it in CAUTION. The rest of the mega-caps are either extreme-extension (AMD, INTC, MU, TSEM, LITE) or hard-blocked (NVDA, TSM, AVGO). Earnings from AMD and LITE tomorrow could reshape the near-term landscape.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — uniform CAUTION across mega-caps
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX slipped 0.6% to 462.06 as AMD pulled back 5.6% ahead of its earnings print. Micron surged 6.8% to a new all-time high, further stretching its extreme extension.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is split between extreme extensions (AMZN +20.4%, GOOG +20.3%) and a more contained MSFT (+4.3%). Palantir's strong Q1 revenue print was overshadowed by its 78x PE and the broader oil-driven risk-off tone. The hyperscaler AI capex narrative remains the structural backbone, but current price levels offer no entry points.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — earnings reactions provide a potential reset
big_tech.key_development: Palantir reported Q1 revenue up 85% YoY after the close, but the stock remains blocked by valuation. Amazon crossed the >20% extreme extension threshold, moving it firmly out of consideration.

interconnected.name: Coherent (COHR)
interconnected.reason: Received a matching $2B investment from NVDA for AI optics alongside LITE — data-center interconnect buildout is a multi-year theme.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's earnings tomorrow confirm AI optics acceleration above 40% QoQ revenue growth.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz oil crisis: UAE detects missiles from Iran; US Central Command denies warship strike, but tanker traffic remains disrupted (Day 57).
US-China trade escalation: New tariffs and sanctions intensify the tech decoupling, with the MATCH Act threatening ASML's China DUV revenue and export controls reshaping semiconductor supply chains.
Fed hawkish pause: US10Y at 4.45% and sticky inflation from energy costs push rate cuts further into the future, pressuring tech valuations.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil elevated on Hormuz keeps WTI above $100, benefiting defense names like PLTR but squeezing consumer and logistics. The rate environment continues to compress tech multiples, with the Semis cluster stuck in uniform CAUTION. Singapore banks face dual NIM compression with no relief from the MAS or US10Y.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: UAE Ministry of Defense reported detecting four missiles from Iran, escalating the Hormuz crisis. US Central Command denied a warship strike, adding to confusion.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Remain in cash; with eleven of seventeen names hard-blocked on extension and two major earnings binaries tomorrow, the risk/reward for new entries is unfavourable — wait for post-earnings price discovery or a macro-driven pullback.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Thirteen of 17 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, with two major earnings binaries tomorrow adding event risk. Cash remains the preferred position.
